finding: Novel Yersinia sp. nov. undetectable by single-gene markers
ani_result: ANI < 95% with Y. intermedia (confirmed distinct species)
interpretation: The circumpolar novel Yersinia sp. nov. is a cryptic species: all 5 marker genes (rpoB, gyrB, recA, atpD, fusA) place it within Y. intermedia despite genome-wide ANI confirming species-level separation. Surveillance approaches relying solely on marker genes would systematically misclassify this species as Y. intermedia, rendering it invisible to current surveillance frameworks.
implication: Genus-wide pangenomic approaches are required to detect cryptic species-level diversity in Yersinia. Marker gene-based surveillance underestimates true species richness in environmental niches.
paper_relevance: Validates title and central hypothesis of Paper 1 (Nature Microbiology)
